comment.body: Today, Thurs OPEX) is the something burger — the YLD complex sells new at-the-money calls, adding negative delta (selling is also executed throughout the day at VWAP). That flow typically picks up around 11:00–11:30 AM ET and can weigh on the tape into midday

comment.body: I saw a very interesting interview with Rick Rieder from Blackrock yesterday on Bloomberg TV, for some of you who missed it, here's the link. This news about BlackRock is significant. What info do they have from their "Aladdin Investment Analysis System"? Black Rock's plays a dominant role in flow dynamics across both fixed income and equities. A reduction in their U.S. equity exposure has a clear knock-on effect—it weakens the persistent, supportive bid that’s underpinned stocks for years. BlackRock, in particular, holds major influence over the RIA community, which still maintains substantial exposure to U.S. equities. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/videos/2025-04-16/rick-rieder-be-tactical-in-jumpy-uncertain-markets-video
